samples.xml_description:
<module id="fsm">
    <description>Diversify FSMs. Include a state diagram. Describe implementation in behavioral Verilog only.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="in" width_description="[31:0]"></input>
        <input id="clk"></input>
        <input id="rst"></input>
        <output id="match" width_description="[31:0]"></output> 
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="s0" value_description="1">Initial state</parameter>
        <parameter id="s1" value_description="0">State 1</parameter>
        <parameter id="s2" value_description="0">State 2</parameter>
        <parameter id="s3" value_description="0">State 3</parameter>
        <parameter id="s4" value_description="0">State 4</parameter>
        <parameter id="s5" value_description="0">State 5</parameter>
        <parameter id="s6" value_description="0">State 6</parameter>
        <parameter id="s7" value_description="0">State 7</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <line>reg[3:0] state = s0;</line>
        <line>reg[3:0] next_state;</line>
        <line>reg[3:0] out;</line>
        <sequential_logic>
            <trigger>always @(posedge clk or posedge rst)</trigger>
            <description>Reset state if rst is high. Else, update state and output.</description>
            <if>
                <condition>rst==1</condition>
                <true>
                    <line>state <= s0;</line>
                    <line>out <= 0;</line>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <line>state <= next_state;</line>
                    <line>out <= next_state == s7 ? 1 : 0;</line>
                </false>
            </if>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic>
            <trigger>always @(in or state)</trigger>
            <description>Determine next state based on current state and input.</description>
            <if>
                <condition>state==s0 && in==1</condition>
                <true>
                    <line>next_state <= s1;</line>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <line>next_state <= state;</line>
                </false>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>state==s1 && in==0</condition>
                <true>
                    <line>next_state <= s2;</line>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <line>next_state <= state;</line>
                </false>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>state==s2 && in==0</condition>
                <true>
                    <line>next_state <= s3;</line>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <line>next_state <= state;</line>
                </false>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>state==s3 && in==1</condition>
                <true>
                    <line>next_state <= s4;</line>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <line>next_state <= state;</line>
                </false>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>state==s4 && in==1</condition>
                <true>
                    <line>next_state <= s5;</line>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <line>next_state <= state;</line>
                </false>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>state==s5 && in==0</condition>
                <true>
                    <line>next_state <= s6;</line>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <line>next_state <= state;</line>
                </false>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>state==s6 && in==0</condition>
                <true>
                    <line>next_state <= s7;</line>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <line>next_state <= state;</line>
                </false>
            </if>
            <if>
                <condition>state==s7 && in==1</condition>
                <true>
                    <line>next_state <= s1;</line>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <line>next_state <= s0;</line>
                </false>
            </if>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The module implements a Mealy state machine that detects the sequence "10011" in the input IN. 
        The state transitions are determined by the input IN and the current state. 
        When the sequence "10011" is detected, the output MATCH is set to 1. 
        In other cases, MATCH is set to 0. 
        The state machine has five states: s0, s1, s2, s3, and s4. 
        The state machine resets to s0 when the reset signal RST is high. 
        The output MATCH is 1 when the state machine is in state s4 and the input IN is 1. 
        The state machine transitions between states based on the input IN and the current state. 
    </implementation>
</module>
